rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HEVENER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Irwin¿s Automotive Enterprises, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 3 (false representation) and 5 (punitive damages) and denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability) and 4 (loss of consortium). The declarations of Terrance Hevener and Mr. Ay create a triable issue whether Richard Hevener was exposed to asbestos-containing Bendix brakes attributable to defendant. Because the Heveners were married prior to Mr. Hevener¿s suffered from an asbestos-related disease, the loss of consortium claimed is not barred due to the date of the marriage. Plaintiffs concede the lack of merit to their claims for false representation and punitive damages. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HEVENER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HEVENER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Ward Ryder Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present admissible evidence that the Hamilton ¿red dot¿ joint compound that was disturbed by defendant in the vicinity of Mr. Hevener contained asbestos. The objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. An expert may not base his opinions on inadmissible deposition testimony and interrogatory responses without some showing that the expert¿s expertise enables him to do something more than merely recite or convey the substance of deposition testimony and interrogatory responses. If the rule were otherwise, the use of an expert in this capacity would vitiate the rules regarding the admissibility of deposition testimony and interrogatory responses. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HEVENER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Alta Building Material Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Dixson¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Hevener was exposed to raw asbestos attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Dixson¿s declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD LANE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its motion for summary adjudication is granted as to plaintiffs¿ claims for strict liability and false representation and denied as to the general negligence claim. The declaration of Mr. Cantley creates a triable issue whether Mr. Lane was exposed to asbestos-containing drywall accessory products attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Cantley¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiffs concede the lack of merit to their claims for strict liability and false representation. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD LANE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Anderson Rowe & Buckley Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. By failing to present plaintiffs¿ responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Lane was exposed to asbestos-containing materials or products attributable to defendant. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEROY CABRAL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cabral was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s failure to address any of plaintiff¿s work locations other than Standard Oil in its separate statement precludes the invocation of the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL SHROPSHIRE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant O¿Connor Lumber Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The only ¿facts¿ set forth in defendant¿s separate statement regarding the purported absence of asbestos in the FixAll that Mr. Shropshire assertedly purchased from defendant are based on inadmissible interrogatory responses and thus defendant proffers no facts supported by admissible evidence on this issue. Plaintiffs¿ objections to defendant¿s use of Dowman¿s GO 129 interrogatory responses are sustained. =(220/HEK)
